references.title: Student Performance Prediction via Attention-Based Multi-Layer Long-Short Term Memory

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Background information is one-hot encoded so that, for each student-course pair, there is a OHE vector. 

LMS data is passed through 3 LSTMs  and the resulting state is used to create an Attention Layer.

These are concatenated and then passed through a Fully connected layer before outputting a prediction.
extracted.Models: Logistic Regression, MLP, LSTM, DOPP, AML
extracted.Comments:
Authors propose a new attention-based model to predict student performance on OULAD. This model, named AML creates an Attention Layer from the LMS logs. 

As time moves forward, so does the predictive capability of the model - past week 15 AML outperforms all benchmarks
extracted.Limitations:
• course information, students’ pre-course learning conditions, and timing of classroom test submissions not used

  • Weak cold start scenario

  • Results are not impressive vs baselines

  • Only uses OULAD

  • No interpretation of “interpretability” benefits 

  • Feature set undisclosed

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Background information is one-hot encoded so that, for each student-course pair, there is a OHE vector. 

LMS data is passed through 3 LSTMs  and the resulting state is used to create an Attention Layer.

These are concatenated and then passed through a Fully connected layer before outputting a prediction.
extracted.Models: Logistic Regression, MLP, LSTM, DOPP, AML
extracted.Comments:
Authors propose a new attention-based model to predict student performance on OULAD. This model, named AML creates an Attention Layer from the LMS logs. 

As time moves forward, so does the predictive capability of the model.

In the binary case, AML always achieves higher accuracy and F1 even though does not always have the highest precision or recall.
extracted.Limitations:
• course information, students’ pre-course learning conditions, and timing of classroom test submissions not used

  • Weak cold start scenario

  • Results are not impressive vs baselines

  • Only uses OULAD

  • No interpretation of “interpretability” benefits 

  • Feature set undisclosed

annotations.text: Over the years, there has been an increase in use of early warning systems to detect students at risk in Online Education: often using VLE interactions and student demographic traits.

Authors propose a new Attention based Multi Layer LSTM model in the hopes of improving predictive accuracy as soon as possible. On the dataset used (undisclosed), authors were able to obtain improvements in performance across different metrics between Weeks 5 to 25.
